docs.title: Phase 5: Form Research Problem and Title
docs.text:
Status: not ready

We will form the final topic only after:

  • the 35 papers are theme-classified
  • source quality is verified
  • Indian/lifestyle data evidence is checked carefully
  • repeated gaps are stable

Current working direction:

Explainable and personalized clinical decision support for IVF outcome prediction and treatment recommendation using multimodal data.

docs.title: Phase 2 Expansion Summary
docs.text: Phase 2 expanded the evidence base from 27 to 35 papers.

docs.title: Papers Added
docs.text: No Paper Why Added 28 Illingworth et al., 2024, Nature Medicine High-value RCT evidence for deep learning embryo selection. It shows AI did not demonstrate noninferiority for clinical pregnancy but improved evaluation speed. 29 Sengupta et al., 2025 Indian subpopulation IVF prediction paper. Useful for Indian validation and population-specific modeling angle. 30 Peng et al., 2024, Scientific Reports ART live-birth prediction using clinical indicators. Useful for clinical-variable prediction and standardization gaps. 31 Endometriosis fresh embryo transfer ML paper, 2025 Disease-specific prediction and personalization evidence. 32 Cohen et al., 2025, Reproductive BioMedicine Online Critical review separating realistic AI value from overclaiming. Useful for trustworthy AI framing. 33 Explainable ML for live birth versus miscarriage after IVF-ET, 2025 Supports XAI and external-validation gap in post-transfer risk assessment. 34 Zhang et al., 2025, Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics Broad review of AI applications in IVF. Useful for trend analysis and workflow framing. 35 Shoham et al., 2025, Journal of IVF-Worldwide Survey evidence on AI adoption by fertility specialists. Useful for deployment and clinician-usability context.

docs.title: What Changed
docs.text:
The expansion strengthened the evidence base in three ways:

  1. It added prospective/RCT evidence, not only retrospective prediction studies.
  2. It added an Indian-population paper, directly relevant to the PhD context.
  3. It added deployment/adoption and critical-review papers, which support trustworthy clinical decision-support framing.

docs.title: Updated Strongest Gaps
docs.text: Gap Frequency Need external validation 26 Need clinical decision support 18 Need real-world deployment evidence 9 Retrospective design 9 Trustworthy AI gap 8 Need prospective/RCT validation 7 Single-center study 6

docs.title: Supervisor Interpretation
docs.text:
The topic direction is now more defensible:

Explainable and personalized clinical decision support for IVF outcome prediction and treatment recommendation.

However, the final title should still wait until theme-wise review pages are written and the Indian/lifestyle data angle is checked carefully.

docs.title: Phase 3 Theme Summary
docs.text: Phase 3 organized the 35-paper evidence base into six review themes.

docs.title: Theme Pages Created
docs.text: Theme Main Use IVF Outcome Prediction Broad prediction evidence base; shows why prediction alone is not enough. Embryo Selection and Grading Supports multimodal embryo-related AI, but also shows black-box and clinical utility risks. Ovarian Stimulation CDSS Strongest practical decision-support theme. FET, Endometrium and Ultrasound Supports multimodal clinical-imaging prediction. XAI and Trustworthy AI Intellectual core for the PhD contribution. Indian and Lifestyle Data Potential local novelty, but needs careful evidence and data feasibility checking.

docs.title: Main Finding
docs.text:
The literature does not support a title that is only:

Machine learning model for IVF success prediction.

That would be too common.

The stronger direction is:

Explainable, personalized and clinically usable IVF decision support.

docs.title: Best Topic Direction After Phase 3
docs.text: Explainable and personalized clinical decision support for IVF outcome prediction and treatment recommendation using multimodal clinical, embryology and contextual data.

docs.title: Next Phase
docs.text:
Phase 4 should shortlist only the repeated gaps that can become a research problem:

  • external validation
  • clinical decision support
  • trustworthy/XAI
  • Indian/contextual validation
  • multimodal clinical + embryology + lifestyle data
  • real-world usability

docs.title: Phase 4 Gap Summary
docs.text: Phase 4 reduced the raw gap list into a PhD-ready gap shortlist.

docs.title: Final Gap Shortlist
docs.text: Gap Role in Topic External validation and generalizability Methodological foundation Clinical decision-support translation Practical contribution Trustworthy and explainable AI Core informatics contribution Multimodal personalization Data/modeling contribution Indian/contextual validation Local relevance Prospective or real-world usability evaluation Clinical usefulness

docs.title: What We Should Not Do
docs.text:
We should not make the final topic:

Machine learning model for IVF success prediction.

That is too generic and already common.

docs.title: What We Should Do
docs.text:
The topic should focus on:

Explainable and personalized clinical decision support for IVF outcome prediction.

docs.title: Strongest Working Title
docs.text: An Explainable and Personalized Clinical Decision Support Framework for IVF Outcome Prediction Using Multimodal Clinical and Embryological Data

docs.title: Conditional Title Extensions
docs.text:
Use these only if data access supports them:

  • in Indian Women
  • using Clinical, Embryological and Lifestyle Data
  • with External Validation in Indian IVF Settings

docs.text:
Phase 5 should now turn this into a formal PhD proposal structure:

  1. final research problem
  2. research questions

  5. proposed methodology
  6. expected contribution

docs.title: Phase 5 Proposal Summary
docs.text: Phase 5 converts the literature and gap analysis into a proposal-ready direction.

docs.title: Recommended Working Title
docs.text: An Explainable and Personalized Clinical Decision Support Framework for IVF Outcome Prediction Using Multimodal Clinical and Embryological Data

docs.title: Why This Title Is Stronger
docs.text:
It includes the repeated gaps:

  • explainable AI
  • personalization
  • clinical decision support
  • IVF outcome prediction
  • multimodal data
  • framework contribution

It avoids weak framing such as:

  • only classifier comparison
  • only accuracy improvement
  • only generic IVF success prediction

docs.title: Conditional Title Options
docs.text:
If Indian clinical data is confirmed:

An Explainable and Personalized Clinical Decision Support Framework for IVF Outcome Prediction Using Multimodal Clinical and Embryological Data in Indian Women

If lifestyle data is confirmed:

An Explainable Multimodal Clinical Decision Support Framework for Personalized IVF Outcome Prediction Using Clinical, Embryological and Lifestyle Data

docs.title: Next Work
docs.text:
Before freezing the final title, confirm:

  1. What IVF dataset can be accessed?
  2. Does the dataset include embryo variables?
  3. Does the dataset include lifestyle variables?
  4. Is there more than one clinic/source for validation?

The answer to these questions determines whether the final title can include Indian, lifestyle, or external validation.

docs.text:
The project follows this sequence:

docs.title: Current Stage
docs.text:
We are between:

  • literature matrix building
  • source cleaning
  • theme classification
  • repeated-gap shortlisting

The title is not final yet.

docs.title: Operating Principles
docs.text:
• Prefer 2021-2025 papers.
  • Prefer Scopus, Web of Science, Elsevier, Springer, IEEE, Wiley and MDPI where relevant.
  • Extract abstract, conclusion, discussion, limitations and future work first.
  • Read full papers only for high-relevance papers.
  • Mark unknown fields as pending instead of guessing.
  • Keep Excel as the structured evidence base.
  • Use this doc site as the research dashboard and thesis-planning workspace.

docs.title: Inclusion Criteria
docs.text:
• AI, ML, DL, XAI or CDSS applied to IVF, ART, embryo selection, ovarian stimulation, FET, endometrial receptivity, live birth, pregnancy or miscarriage prediction.
  • Published mainly from 2021 to 2025.
  • Peer-reviewed journal preferred.
  • Review papers allowed only for trend and gap synthesis.
  • Preprints allowed only as supporting evidence, not final novelty proof.

docs.title: Exclusion Criteria
docs.text:
• Papers unrelated to IVF or ART.
  • Purely biological papers without informatics, AI, ML or decision-support relevance.
  • Non-academic clinic blogs or promotional sources.
  • Papers with no usable abstract, method, dataset or limitation details.

docs.title: Extraction Fields
docs.text:
Each paper should be extracted into:

  • paper number
  • title
  • authors
  • year
  • journal
  • objective
  • dataset
  • features used
  • method/algorithm
  • technology used
  • evaluation metrics
  • main findings
  • limitations
  • future work
  • gap found
  • category
  • relevance
  • source URL
  • extraction confidence

docs.title: Quality Flags
docs.text:
Use these confidence levels:

Confidence Meaning High Publisher/full text/PMC/PubMed verified and key fields extracted Medium Abstract and useful extraction available, but full limitations need checking Low Bibliographic or limitation details incomplete

docs.title: Gap Rule
docs.text:
A gap becomes important only when it is:

  • repeated across multiple papers
  • relevant to IVF practice
  • technically addressable by Computer Applications research
  • feasible with obtainable data
  • not already solved by a strong validated system
